items.speaker: Suspicious Figure
items.text: What's taking you so long?

items.speaker: Suspicious Figure
items.text: If we don't bring back the goods, the boss will have our heads！

items.speaker: Suspicious Figure
items.text: Ugh... Oi, best keep your nose out of our business. We're with the Black Alley.

items.speaker: Cautious Figure
items.text: Idiot! {Male=He's;Female=She's} got the stance of a skilled fighter. We need to move!
Excuse me, I… I need your help.

items.speaker: Familiar Voice
items.text: Hey, what's got you two running scared?
Huh?!

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: A flurry of panicked sounds echo in the distance, and the sense of being watched fades.

options.text: Zani?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: {PlayerName}？

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Do you know what these two were up to?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: They were babbling about "hostages" and "feints," and then they came at me.

options.text: They were trying to break into someone's home.
Let's just say they forgot to bring their house key.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: And they just ran after taking one look at you, hm? Seems about right.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Thieves, you mean? I can imagine why they were fleeing like that.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I have. But that name… it's already...

items.speaker: Serious Acolyte
items.text: You there! What's going on here?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Just two thieves. Took care of them. I was about to deliver them to the <te href=800005>Chamber of Discipline</te> myself.

items.speaker: Serious Acolyte
items.text: What about {Male=him;Female=her}?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Same as you, drawn here by the sound of a scuffle.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I know the rules. I'll go with you, make the report, and be done with it.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let me handle this. You'd rather not deal with their questions, hm?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So there was somebody else...

items.speaker: Serious Acolyte
items.text: Right, you'll come with me to the Chamber then.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: This might be more complicated than it looks. Will you wait for me?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: This won't take long.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Aside from a few broken rocks, only silence greets your efforts.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Strange... my memory shouldn't be failing me, should it?

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: Who's causing trouble here?! Don't you know this is the Masked Brothers' turf?

items.speaker: Masked Little Brother
items.text: B-Brother... they look tough...

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: What's there to be afraid of? We are the ones rebuilding the Black Alley!

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: Now, how about you hand over some coin, eh?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Well, that was a wasted trip.

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: Wait, mercy! We were blind. Please just let us go this time!

items.speaker: Masked Little Brother
items.text: Told you, didn't I? Told you they were tough! Why do you never listen to me?

items.speaker: Masked Little Brother
items.text: Is this place cursed? Every time something happens, it's never good news.

items.speaker: Masked Little Brother
items.text: Just like the last time...

options.text: Hold on. Last time?

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: Yeah, weeks ago. There was one hell of a racket. We thought the whole place was falling apart.

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: Turns out, it was a bloody terror of a woman. Saw us, didn't say a word, just pulled a blade on us.

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: We were lucky to get away. Took us two weeks to come back.

options.text: She's the one who wrecked the entrance, then?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Perhaps... but the real question is why.

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: Then... everything's good now, right? We'll just be on our way.

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: Quietly...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Is that all the nerve you've got? And you call yourselves the ones to bring back the Black Alley?

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: Well you got to have something to make folks fear you, right? One of you looks soft, and the other looks halfway worked to death... I liked my chances.

options.text: Shame you picked the wrong one.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: How many times have you pulled this off?

items.speaker: Masked Big Brother
items.text: Not even once!!!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Save the rest for the Chamber of Discipline.
Zani swiftly subdues the two would-be escapees.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: {PlayerName}, I'll keep digging into the Black Alley and what happened today.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'll let you know when I have something.

options.text: Okay.
You seem surprisingly motivated? That's not normal.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Well, after handing over those other two thieves, I learned they were targeting Montelli staff residences.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Providing a safe working environment is part of my job description too.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It's getting late. We'll pick this up tomorrow, once I've got more to go on.

dialog.stepLabel: 赞妮打电话来，剧情里调到14点
dialog.nodeDesc: 赞妮打电话来，剧情里调到14点

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: {PlayerName}, I've got some updates on the situation.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The name of their leader—Talos.

options.text: I don't know that name.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Mmm... that name should've died along with the Black Alley years ago.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You mentioned before that someone single-handedly wiped out the upper ranks of the Black Alley. Any idea who it was?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Well... the rumors call them... the "Blazing Nightwalker."

options.text: Come again?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The Blazing Nightwalker. Ugh, don't make me say it again.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: If you're free, come by and we'll talk. There's something I could use your help with.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Which one should I pick?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Hmm... I'll take all of them. One of each, please.

options.text: A bit hard on digestion, no?
You ordered too much.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Ah, {PlayerName}, you made it. Let's sit over there and talk.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I wasn't sure what you liked. It was too hard to choose, so I just got one of each flavor.

options.text: I took you for more of a coffee person.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Work is bitter enough as it is. Why drink something bitter on top of that?

options.text: Things not going well?
Those two thieves give you more trouble than expected?
I thought they promised you a vacation?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Yeah... Still about the stuff from last time.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Actually, a lot of the family's staff have been running into the same kind of trouble as that night.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: They're not anything special on their own.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: What matters is... a lot of the family's employees have been running into the same kind of trouble as that night.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: A vacation is like an ad. Only the ones dishing it out have the power to make it real or fake.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: And recently, a lot of the family's employees have been running into the same kind of trouble as that night.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Guess my vacation will just have to be postponed again.

options.text: What if I help you apply for it next time?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Hmm... Maybe that could work.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So, where should we start?

options.text: First, tell me what you need help with.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: As I mentioned before, many people working for the Montellis have been victims of attempted thefts, like last night.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The family has tightened security, but just sitting here waiting won't solve anything.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: As for the ones we caught... they haven't been chatty. Plus, they've only committed a few small-time crimes.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So it would be tricky to use... more extreme methods on them. If we do, it could give the wrong people a chance to twist the situation.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: You mentioned someone asked for your help earlier, right?

options.text: You want to locate her?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: We've been trying but haven't found anyone who matches her description yet. She's probably keeping a low profile.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But right now, she might be the only lead we've got.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So, might I ask you play your face card one more time?

options.text: Seems it's up to me again.
For the ice cream, I guess I can't say no.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Much appreciated.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Maybe some more then?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: We'll do it tonight. It will be in your hands.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Feel free to rest until it's time. I've already made arrangements at a hotel.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: You know what. I'll be here for a while. If you have any questions, just ask away.

options.text: Do I need to prepare anything?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Everything is taken care of.
You take some rest in your room. By the time you wake up, you realize you haven't slept much.
So, you leave the hotel and decide to wander around before the operation starts.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: {PlayerName}? It's not time yet. Aren't you going to rest a little longer?

options.text: Aren't you?
Can't sleep anyway.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I am resting, alright?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'm afraid I can't help you with that.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Insomnia and I go way back.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: What about?

options.text: The family's approach this time.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: A lot of people were targeted, but in the end, they only sent you to handle it?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: If we send too many people, we risk tipping our hand.

options.text: Why not tell The Order?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Well, the Chamber of Discipline did catch a few perps, but they're not quick to act without solid proof.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: And... some of the higher-ups in the Order might just be happy to see the Montellis having a little trouble.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The biggest issue I find is...

options.text: You actually volunteered for extra work?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Really? That's the biggest issue you find?

options.text: There's probably something you're not telling me, isn't there?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Alright, alright... You've got sharp instincts. Spending too much time with you, and I'm running out of secrets.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I was the one who delivered Talos and those Black Alley leaders to The Order.

options.text: What?!
I knew it all along.
The Blazing Nightwalker... So is it the Rinascitan tradition to have a moniker?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Yeah, that's me... the Blazing Nightwalker. But honestly? I really don't want to keep repeating that name.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: By tradition, shouldn't you have revealed your true identity at the end?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Oh, you're talking about the stories where, after a lot of hardship and misunderstanding, one side lets the truth out with their last breath, leaving the other to regret it forever?

options.text: Where did you read that?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I've got my own ways to keep entertained.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Hiding my true identity got old fast. Since you were curious, I might as well just tell you.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: After all, you're not someone I can't trust, right?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Well, as you have already known, I am the Blazing Nightwalker... though I really wish I didn't have to keep repeating that name.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Honestly, it's a bit of a burden...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Oh, and by the way, after you became the Laureate, they're giving you all sorts of monikers too.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The family sent me to handle this, partly because they didn't want a big operation to stir things up, and partly because... well, it was a strategic move.

options.text: Hard for me to imagine you all fired up like that once.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I have to say it again, I never called myself that.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Back then, I was just doing what needed to be done. Never planned on picking up a fancy nickname.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It's hard to imagine that name suiting you now.

options.text: What happened?
Did work change you?
You seem to have lost a spark.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Well, that's what work does to you...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Do you think this "Talos" is the real deal then?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: ...Not sure. Could just be the same old story repeating itself.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let's call it a cleanup job.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: If things go smoothly, maybe we can grab a late-night snack after, hmm?

options.text: I'll go all in then.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I don't think this is serious enough to give it your all.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Just do enough to get it done. But keep some of that enthusiasm for the snacks later, hm?

items.speaker: Cocky Thug
items.text: Ugh... You hit harder than I thought...

options.text: Stop resisting. Just surrender!

items.speaker: Cocky Thug
items.text: Well, my boss gave me this—!

options.text: Zani.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let's move in. Now!

items.speaker: Panicked Thug
items.text: Blimey, how the hell are you movin' so quick?!

items.speaker: Bakie
items.text: *Beep*—*Beep*!

options.text: Put Bakie down. Now!
Stop this pointless resistance!

items.speaker: Panicked Thug
items.text: Not a chance! It's my hostage now... This is an Echonapping, you hear!

items.speaker: Panicked Thug
items.text: Ha! Ain't no way you'll do somethin' stupid while I'm holding this Echo!

items.speaker: Bakie
items.text: Beep! Beep!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Hey! Don't be causing trouble for folks who need their breakfast bright and early.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Alright, start talking. Where's your hideout? Who's the one really calling the shots?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Looks like we'll have to drag him back. We'll get answers sooner or later.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: A shame this one's only good for squealing. He's not the one we're after.
Is it... me you're looking for?

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: My name is Colleen... Please, help me and my friends.

dialog.stepLabel: 衔接Zanni_R01005之后：柯琳解释情况
dialog.nodeDesc: 衔接Zanni_R01005之后：柯琳解释情况

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Before we go any further, shouldn't you explain who you are first?

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: I'm from the Black Alley...

options.text: So you are one of them too?
You don't exactly look like you're in the same business.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: No, no, no... The Black Alley is more than just the gang! It's the place too.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The entrance to the Black Alley is shut. It's done.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: You're right, Talos destroyed it. But there's another way in...

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: It used to be a home for vagrants like us. A few scraps, a couple of jobs... just enough to get by.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: But then she came, and it all went up in smoke...

options.text: What did she do to you?

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: She came in claiming this place was hers since they owned it before.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: Told us, if we wanted to stay, we had to follow her rules. No other choice.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: Steal, lie, cheat, rob... same as the old days. Give her your tribute, or... or you get a beating.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: A fish that slipped the net...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Sounds just like how the Black Alley ran things... But do people actually accept her as the acting Talos?

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: Some people did. They wanted to, you know? The ones with the dirty hands already. They were easy.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: But the main reason is that no one can stand up to her...

options.text: Is she that strong?
Why?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Colleen doesn't answer your question, instead she looks at Zani with a conflicted and wary expression.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'll wait outside.

options.text: No.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: {PlayerName}？

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Colleen, if you can't trust my friend, then I can't offer you my help.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Trust goes both ways. You can't ask for help with one hand while holding a knife with the other.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The operation is a wrap. Bring the ones we caught in and then everyone can call it a day.

items.speaker: Voice in Terminal
items.text: Got it, Ms. Zani. Any leads on your side?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: No, nothing to report.

items.speaker: Voice in Terminal
items.text: Ah, looks like we'll have to try again tomorrow.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It's just us now. Anything else bothering you while I'm at it?

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: A Terminal... Talos also has a Terminal. It lets her transform into a powerful Echo.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So that's it. You are worried the Terminal was supplied by someone from the Montelli family, aren't you?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: That's why you doubt us and prefer to put your faith in a wayfaring Laureate, hm?

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: I'm sorry...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: There's no need to apologize. You're a victim in this, with every right to be suspicious.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So then, {PlayerName}, what do you think we should do?

options.text: What's your take? It's your job, after all.
I can't ignore someone who's asking for help.
I want to see how powerful this Echo really is.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: *Sigh*... Who told everyone to call it a day again?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: As an employee of the Montelli family, I have an obligation to stop any Terminals from being misused.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'll just have to ask the boss for double overtime pay and an extra vacation after this.

options.text: Great, but have you spent the vacation you got last time?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Are you really bringing up something so depressing now?

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: Thank you, thank you both!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Anyway, that's the real story behind that ridiculous name.

options.text: I thought it would be a more elaborate story.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Not everything needs to be turned into some grand tale.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: In this city, it's mostly the same routine every day.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: How long can I snooze before I'm late...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Which time's best to line up to get a fresh wrap...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: How to sleep without lying in bed for hours...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: These little routines are fragile. One bad move and they're gone.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But likewise, just one bold move is enough to bring them back.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Most people got it wrong, slapping that big name on me.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It's just part of my routine. Like snoozing, eating, or keeping my hair and horns in check.

options.text: So, is taking down an entire gang also part of it?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I was alone back then. I didn't even bother hiding who I was.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: And that's when the trouble started to find me.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I thought, why wait for it to get worse? Better to finish it quickly.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But of course, I made it worse... Too fast, too soon.

options.text: So how did you deal with it back then?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The Montelli family reached out to me and offered to clean up the mess. Even said they'd back me up in the future.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So, I changed jobs. The work is the same. No difference.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Disappointed?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Your instincts came from a good place. A fist might not be as sharp as a blade, but it can still break through deeply rooted problems.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: And up close, it's gentle enough to protect what's important.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: So don't let today bother you.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: No, I'm not letting it bother me.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Cleaning up simple scum doesn't weigh on me.

options.text: So what you said back there...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Just reliving some old memories, that's all.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But you're right about something...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: What you said just now applies to you, too.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The difference, though, is that you are a blade. A weapon that can break the cycle.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But you don't seem to realize it. Seeing you has brought some old feelings back to me.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Don't you see? Ever since you helped make the Carnevale happen, Ragunna's been different.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Things are busier now. There's more work, more opportunities.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I told you before that these routines are fragile. One bad move and they're gone, right?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So... a stable environment is the only way to reduce the bad moves. That's the way we get to keep our routines.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I tried to do that for a long time, but you're the one who made it happen.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: With fewer bad actors on the streets these days, <ano=The Blazing Nightwalker>I</ano>... am increasingly irrelevant.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Perhaps one day, I'll finally be able to sleep through the night.

options.text: So, a few nights ago, you were also...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Of course. It was all part of the routine.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I've been thinking... It's a relief that someone like you is willing to step up.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Your potential is probably beyond what anyone can imagine...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Yet, you still insist on being responsible for everything, no matter how big or small.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Thank you. You've done well.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Ah, sorry... It's just a habit...

options.text: It's okay.
I'll take it if I can return the favor.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Hmm... Okay.

options.text: Ruffle her hair.
Touch her horns.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Alright... that's enough.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Hey, you didn't say you were going to do that...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: By the way, this is for you.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It used to be my weapon.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: You can take it as a token of silence, or as part of an agreement.

options.text: An agreement to do just enough to get it done?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Oh, as if I'd do that! That would just be getting in your way.

items.speaker: Runner
items.text: Wait, wait! Wait, wait! You're after the Black Alley's entrance, aren't you?

options.text: I haven't said a word yet...

items.speaker: Runner
items.text: I know my value to people.

items.speaker: Runner
items.text: Now that you've got me, no point in resisting. The longer I hold out, the more pain I'm asking for.

items.speaker: Runner
items.text: Smart people know when to give in. It's how I stayed alive.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: {PlayerName}, have you got him?

options.text: Yeah, and he's already talking.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So we won't need to "help" him talk after all.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Once he coughs up the entrance location, we'll head straight there from here.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The family is almost on site. They can handle the cleanup.
With the description the runner provided, you find the Black Alley's entrance.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Might be a bit late to ask, but… Colleen, you did what Talos asked, didn't you?

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: I didn't...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Did you forget what {PlayerName} said? Trust goes both ways.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: I… I didn't have a choice! If I didn't, they'd... they'd...you know, hurt me!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Then when the time comes, make sure you tell the Chamber of Discipline everything. No lies.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: But… but I…

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Don't excuse yourself by saying you were forced into it. Can you do that?

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: Fine... I get it.

options.text: The Blazing Nightwalker is no joke.
Giving someone a lecture? Are you sure you're Zani?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Ugh, don't call me that.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Believe me, I'd kill to be in a bed right now instead of standing out here.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let's get this over with.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'm praying it doesn't reek in there.

options.text: Please don't point that out right now.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: Don't worry. It may be an underground waterway, but it's been abandoned for a long time now.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: It's not exactly clean, but it's livable. We did some work on it.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: The pipes are a maze, though. They split up the territory by rank...

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: Talos is holed up somewhere deep in the waterway.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: "Demons always live in hell, like the Primus always stays in the Cathedral." Yeah, that's about right.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Oh, and {PlayerName}, masks on.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The mask makes things a lot easier.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It's better if neither of us gets recognized too easily.

options.text: Fair point, but...

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: H-hey… I'll pretend I don't know who you are!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Guess I don't need to spell it out for you.
The runner's men rush out in an attempt to overwhelm Zani with their numbers.
Meanwhile, you intercept the runner attempting to flee.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Here. Sorry to keep you waiting.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Mmm... those Chamber of Discipline folk are as difficult to deal with as ever.

options.text: How did it go?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Well... you know how they are with the Montellis these days. No surprise they didn't share any interrogation details.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I told them the thieves claimed to be with the Black Alley, but the Chamber doesn't bother with rumors without solid proof.

options.text: What exactly is the Black Alley?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: A gang made up of criminals, although how many joined willingly is anyone's guess.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: A lot of vagrants were pressed into their service. Pawns in a game of crime.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The leadership got wiped out a few years ago by someone working solo. The leftovers? They were screwed. Some are still rotting in prison.

options.text: Do you think those two were just a few stragglers?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Maybe. Or maybe they just wanted to spook you... but they picked the wrong person to mess with.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: You're still thinking about that person who asked for help, huh? Got any leads?

options.text: (Tell Zani what you discovered.)

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: You think she's in the same boat as those vagrants in the past? Forced into something they didn't choose?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Ah, {PlayerName}, you're the type who can't turn your back when someone asks for help.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It's not too late. Got time for a detour?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The old entrance to the Black Alley.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: If they're crawling back from the ashes, we might find a clue or two at their old haunt.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: That's his hideout. Over there.

items.speaker: Colleen
items.text: He's a slippery one. One wrong move, and he'll be gone, off in a Gondola before you can blink.

options.text: We can use that to our advantage.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Looks like we're thinking the same thing.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'll cause a distraction up front. Leave the show to me.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: And you'll move to checkmate.
Following the plan, you move to the rear of the hideout. You signal Zani to initiate the operation once in position.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Someone's making moves. But they just seem to be petty thieves.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Got it. You're clear to act when you see fit.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'll watch your back and keep an eye out for anyone trying to shadow you.
